commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared core event-session routing helper and threads it through exec, heartbeat, cron, gateway node-event, ACP parent-stream, and CLI watchdog paths with focused regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible on current main: exec completion and forced heartbeat paths preserve same-agent direct DM keys because only cron-run keys are remapped. The related issue also includes production session-store evidence for the same orphan-session behavior.
commands.review_followup: Keep this in normal PR review because the remaining blocker is proof and maintainer risk acceptance, not a narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: fix(core): route dmScope main event wakes to agent main
target.body:
Fixes #71581.
Replaces #72648 with a narrower implementation.

## Summary
- Add a shared event-session routing helper for internal system-event enqueue and heartbeat wake targets.
- Route single-owner `session.dmScope=main` direct DM event keys back to the drained agent main session instead of leaving events on stale per-DM queues.
- Preserve existing cron-run remapping and explicit route-binding direct-session overrides.
- Apply the helper to exec completion events, gateway node exec events, cron forced-session resolution, heartbeat forced-session resolution, ACP parent-stream events, and CLI watchdog events.

## Duplicate check
Before opening this PR, I searched open PRs for `71581 dmScope exec-event cron-event heartbeat`, `session.dmScope heartbeat`, and `resolveHeartbeatSession resolveEventSessionKey scopedHeartbeatWakeOptions`. No active duplicate appeared other than the abandoned #72648.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed:
- With `session.dmScope=main` and a single static DM owner allowlist, internal exec/cron event wakes can carry an old direct DM session key such as `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`. Those events need to enqueue and wake the agent main session that the heartbeat actually drains.

Real environment tested:
- GCP Crabbox Spot VM, `e2-standard-4`, `us-east1-b`
- Node `24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`
- Commit `887eaae610`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 pnpm check:changed -- --timed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/infra/event-session-routing.test.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.subagent-session-guard.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose
node --import tsx -e "import { resolveEventSessionRoutingPolicy, resolveEventSessionKeyForPolicy, scopedHeartbeatWakeOptionsForPolicy } from './src/infra/event-session-routing.ts'; const cfg={session:{dmScope:'main'},channels:{telegram:{allowFrom:['123']}}}; const policy=resolveEventSessionRoutingPolicy({cfg,sessionKey:'agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123'}); if (resolveEventSessionKeyForPolicy('agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123', policy) !== 'agent:main:main') throw new Error('event key did not route to main'); const wake=scopedHeartbeatWakeOptionsForPolicy('agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123',{reason:'exec-event'},policy); if (wake.sessionKey !== 'agent:main:main') throw new Error('wake did not route to main'); console.log('event-session-routing smoke ok');"
```

Evidence after fix:
- `check:changed` summary was clean for conflict markers, changelog attributions, extension export guards, duplicate coverage, dependency pins, package patches, core typecheck, core test typecheck, core lint, runtime sidecar loader guard, runtime import cycles, webhook body guard, pairing store guard, and pairing account guard.
- Targeted Vitest result: `4 passed`, `87 passed`.
- Runtime smoke printed `event-session-routing smoke ok`.

Observed result after fix:
- Single-owner `dmScope=main` direct event keys resolve to `agent:main:main` for enqueue and heartbeat wake.
- Global session scope still wakes the agent while enqueueing on `global`.
- Multi-owner and wildcard allowlists do not collapse direct keys.
- Explicit route-binding direct-session overrides are preserved.

What was not tested:
- A full local WSL Vitest run; local Vitest stalled before output on this host, so validation was run remotely.
- `pnpm check:changed` without `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1`; the raw VM hung in unrelated plugin SDK boundary-artifact lint preparation, so the final remote gate skipped only that preparation step while still running full core lint.
